title: Homepage enhancements, timer page, bottom nav bar, and motivational target widget

requirements.summary: Display a 'Days Left to End of Month' countdown banner at the very top of the homepage (Dashboard)
acceptanceCriteria: The days-left count is shown at the top of the Dashboard page
The number updates correctly as the date changes
The banner is visually distinct and easy to read

file_operations.description: Add a countdown banner component at the top of the Dashboard that calculates and displays the number of days remaining in the current calendar month. Use JavaScript Date API to compute days left (e.g., days in month minus current day). Style the banner to be visually prominent with appropriate spacing and typography.

requirements.summary: On the homepage (Dashboard), show a section that lists the user's upcoming reminders and active targets
acceptanceCriteria: Reminders and targets added by the user appear on the homepage
Each item shows its title and due date/time
Section updates reactively when items are added or removed

file_operations.description: Add a new section below existing dashboard content that displays upcoming reminders and active targets. Use existing useGetReminders and useGetTargets hooks from frontend/src/hooks/useQueries.ts to fetch data from localStorage. Filter to show only incomplete/future items. Display each item with its title, deadline/time, and status. Ensure the section updates reactively when the underlying data changes.

requirements.summary: Add a calendar-clock icon button in the top-right area of the homepage header, positioned beside the existing message icon
acceptanceCriteria: A calendar-clock icon is visible in the top-right header beside the message icon
Tapping the icon navigates to the Timer page
The icon is accessible on all screen sizes

file_operations.description: Add a calendar-clock icon button in the top-right header section of the Dashboard page, positioned next to the existing message navigation button. Use lucide-react icons (CalendarClock). Wire the button to navigate to the '/timer' route using TanStack Router's Link or useNavigate. Ensure the icon is responsive and accessible.

requirements.summary: Create a new Timer/Countdown page with add, display, and delete functionality for multiple timers
acceptanceCriteria: User can open an 'Add Timer' form with start (month/day) and end (year/month/day) inputs
Saved timers display a large live countdown in months, days, hours, minutes
Multiple timers can be active simultaneously
Timers persist across page refreshes (localStorage)
Each timer can be deleted individually

file_operations.description: Create a new Timer page component that allows users to add timers with start date (month, day) and end date (year, month, day) fields. Display all active timers with large, prominent countdown text showing months, days, hours, and minutes remaining. Implement real-time countdown updates using setInterval. Support multiple simultaneous timers displayed as a list. Include a delete button for each timer. Use localStorage to persist timer data (integrate with frontend/src/utils/localStorageService.ts).

Register the new '/timer' route in the TanStack Router configuration. Import TimerPage and add it as a child route under the authenticated studentRoute. Ensure the route is protected by the student auth guard.

Add timer data storage methods to localStorageService: addTimer, getTimers, deleteTimer. Define a Timer interface with fields for id, label, startDate (month, day), endDate (year, month, day), and createdAt. Store timers in the user-namespaced localStorage key pattern used by other entities.

requirements.summary: Add a persistent bottom navigation bar visible on every page with five buttons: Home, Subject, Question, Reminder, and Target
acceptanceCriteria: Bottom navigation bar is visible and fixed on every authenticated page
Five buttons: Home, Subject, Question, Reminder, Target — each with an icon and label
Each button navigates correctly to the respective page
Active page button is highlighted
The bar does not overlap main page content (page content has bottom padding)

file_operations.description: Create a BottomNavBar component with five navigation buttons: Home (to /), Subject (to /subjects), Question (to /question-bank), Reminder (to /reminders), and Target (to /targets). Each button includes an icon from lucide-react and a label. Use TanStack Router's Link component and useRouterState or useLocation to detect the active route and highlight the corresponding button. Style the bar to be fixed at the bottom of the viewport with appropriate z-index and background.

Import and render the BottomNavBar component at the bottom of the Layout component, below the main content area. Add bottom padding to the main content wrapper (e.g., pb-20 or pb-24) to prevent content from being hidden behind the fixed bottom nav bar.

requirements.summary: Add a 'Target 🎯 100%' motivational widget on the homepage (Dashboard) with a bold winner aesthetic
acceptanceCriteria: A Target 🎯 widget is visible on the homepage
It displays the user's current target completion percentage
The widget uses gold, trophy, or championship-themed colors and design
It includes a motivational message encouraging 100% completion
The widget updates when targets are completed

file_operations.description: Create a TargetMotivationWidget component that displays a motivational target completion widget. Calculate the completion percentage from the user's targets (completed / total). Use bold, champion-themed styling with gold/trophy colors (e.g., gradient backgrounds with yellows, oranges, and golds), large trophy or medal icons, and energetic typography. Include the 🎯 emoji and a motivational message encouraging the user to reach 100%. Make the design visually striking and celebratory.

Import and render the TargetMotivationWidget component prominently on the Dashboard page (e.g., near the top after the countdown banner or in a featured section). Ensure it receives target data via the useGetTargets hook and reactively updates when targets change.
